name: Zeppole
recipeInstructions:
Zeppole comes from the word zeppelin. Zeppole means fried "puffs". When cooked properly, they should be heavenly and so light you might expect them to float away. This particular version comes from Naples, Italy. There are many zeppole recipes; this one is more of a fried puff pastry opposed to other zeppoles which are more breadlike.
1. Mix water, sugar, oil, and bay leaf in a heavy saucepan and bring to a boil.
2. Mix flour and semolina together and add them into the boiling mixture all at once.
3. Remove from heat and begin stirring with a wooden spoon until it forms a mass.
4. Return to the lowest heat and cook for 5 minutes, stirring continuously.
5. Remove from heat, discard bay leaf, and add yolks one at a time, beating vigorously after each addition.
6. Add Marsala wine and beat dough until smooth.
7. You can use a food processor to prepare this dough if you wish to make the above steps go faster.
8. Grease a countertop or board with olive oil and pat down dough into a pancake.
9. Flatten dough and fold sides over on itself so there are 3 layers.
10. Flatten dough again and repeat folding 5 more times.
11. Take about 1/3 of the dough and roll it out to a thickness of your thumb, making each piece into a ring.
12. Make 3 or 4 rings in total and slip them into boiling oil.
13. When turning, prick the rings with a sharp knife or fork in a few places.
14. Drain on brown paper and serve warm, dusted with powdered sugar.
